summary: Exact HEAD 56d69700700016747741657b3af580bab5991777 passes release-risk review. The release candidate adds no database or external-data migration, preserves legacy and disable-autonomous rollback paths, persists typed provider/auth/quota/permission/timeout/cancel and repair-convergence stops with recovery metadata, reuses the merged production connector and independent-review owners, and keeps PR creation, merge, waiver, deployment, publication, and material external effects under explicit human authority. Exact-head full suite 1903/1903, targeted suite 290/290, integration and production-shaped E2E 4/4, build/typecheck evidence, clean diff check, and target conformance 80 versus origin/main baseline 81 with zero new run-session-to-CLI reverse dependencies support advancement. This role verdict is not Gate or merge authorization: current pr-prepare correctly remains not ready until the active current-head reviews are closed and recorded, adjudication and validation sequencing are rebound, Gate is regenerated, current-head CI is imported, and execute merge is explicitly invoked.
inspection_summary: Inspected the strict-head release-risk request; clean Git state and origin/main diff; completed final Story and parent roadmap with staged post-PR closure; predecessor PR lineage; Architecture, canonical Spec, test plan, and execution-topology replay; the public CLI and run-session composition root; production connector and independent-review reuse; managed-worktree authority; current-head final-prepare binding; typed provider and lifecycle stops; bounded needs_changes repair and fresh review; durable Run compatibility and migration coverage; exact-head verification, full, targeted, integration, E2E, build, typecheck, conformance, review lifecycle, sequencing, and pr-prepare artifacts. No release-risk regression or authority-boundary violation was found. The remaining non-ready Gate state is expected coordination work and must remain fail closed until recorded.
inspection_inputs: git rev-parse HEAD; git status --short; git diff --check origin/main...HEAD; git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD; git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD
judgment_delta: Initial concern: post-PR completed Story and roadmap state might overstate delivery or erase the explicit merge boundary -> Final judgment: the Story records staged delivery, traces PR #385 separately, and still requires current-head Gate, CI import, and explicit vibepro execute merge; completion metadata does not grant runtime merge authority.

Initial concern: the final composition might duplicate Production Runtime Connectors or Independent Review Orchestration -> Final judgment: codex-runtime-bridge imports createProductionRuntimeConnectors and guarded-run-session imports createGuardedIndependentReviewRunner; the new closure owner composes these predecessors and adds no replacement service.

Initial concern: a one-command mutation path could leak PR, merge, waiver, deploy, publish, or material external authority -> Final judgment: implementation and review prompts explicitly prohibit those effects, implementation uses workspace-write local-only/no-network authority, reviews are read-only, negative E2E assertions exclude forbidden actions, and final_prepare is the terminal automated authority.

Initial concern: provider or asynchronous failure could leave an ambiguous partially-mutated Run -> Final judgment: runtime adapter and Run persistence retain provider, missing capability, recovery and resume details; tests cover pre-mutation capability stop, auth, quota, permission, unavailable provider, start/status/result timeout, cancellation precedence, stale completion, corruption, future schema, verification failure, CI pending, repeated needs_changes, and no-progress.

Initial concern: durable Run schema or rollout state could require an unproven migration -> Final judgment: this change introduces no database or external-data migration; inherited state migration, mirror repair, corruption quarantine, future-schema preservation, and legacy status compatibility are covered, while --action-profile legacy and --disable-autonomous-actions provide auditable rollback.

Initial concern: the release surface might worsen architecture or broad regressions -> Final judgment: exact-head full tests pass 1903/1903, targeted tests pass 290/290, integration and production-shaped E2E pass 4/4, diff check is clean, and conformance improves total violations from 81 to 80 with zero new run-session-to-CLI reverse dependencies.

Initial concern: stale prior strict-head reviews and invalidated sequencing might be mistaken for a release pass -> Final judgment: this independent verdict is bound to 56d69700 and passes the release-risk role only; pr-prepare remains correctly fail closed until current reviews, adjudication, sequencing, Gate regeneration, current-head CI import, and explicit merge are completed.
